football.headline: Arsenal lost 1-2 to Bournemouth at the Emirates, with Alex Scott scoring the winner 16 minutes from time, leaving Arsenal's lead over Manchester City at 9 points with a trip to the Etihad next Sunday looming
Barcelona beat Espanyol in the El Clasico de Catalunya derby, with Lamine Yamal taunting rivals on Instagram afterwards, as Barca close in on the LaLiga title
Liverpool beat Fulham 2-0, with 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha scoring on only his second Premier League start and assisting Mohamed Salah's goal, ending Liverpool's run without a league win since February
West Ham beat Wolves 4-0 at home, with Jarrod Bowen among the scorers, as Wolves remain deep in relegation trouble
Sevilla beat Atletico de Madrid in LaLiga, with Nemanja Gudelj scoring a header from a corner and Sergio Ramos publicly celebrating the victory on social media
Brentford drew 2-2 with Everton in the Premier League, with Jordan Pickford saving Everton's point in a game Brentford led
Erling Haaland leads the Premier League Golden Boot race with 22 goals, one ahead of Brentford's Igor Thiago on 21, with Antoine Semenyo third on 15

australia.headline: Coalition backs Labor's crackdown on NDIS providers as Health Minister Mark Butler prepares to unveil savings plan

archaeology.headline: Reanalysis of genomic bottleneck evidence questions whether Homo sapiens came close to extinction around 900,000 years ago, casting doubt on the severe population crash proposed in a widely cited 2023 study

Genetic analysis of Neanderthal remains shows population decline predated contact with modern humans, suggesting internal demographic fragility rather than competitive exclusion drove their extinction

Stone tool assemblage shift recorded at African sites dated to approximately 200,000 years ago linked to environmental and social network changes rather than cognitive leaps, according to new behavioural analysis

Skeletal analysis of Australopithecus specimens indicates the genus experienced obstructed labour analogous to modern humans, pushing the origin of difficult birth further back than 2 million years ago

australia.headline: Coalition releases migration policy proposing Australian values test for visa applicants, with Angus Taylor spruiking the plan

archaeology.headline: New Scientist reports genetic analysis may overturn the hypothesis that early Homo sapiens experienced a near-extinction bottleneck, suggesting population history was more complex than previously modelled

Genetic study of Neanderthal remains identifies demographic signals of prolonged population decline preceding their disappearance approximately 40,000 years ago, with low diversity consistent with small isolated groups

Australopithecus pelvic morphology analysis indicates birth canal constraints produced difficult parturition comparable to modern humans, pushing the obstetric dilemma back at least 2-3 million years before the genus Homo

Stone tool assemblage shift 200,000 years ago in early Homo sapiens sites attributed to new environmental or social pressures rather than simple cultural drift, according to fresh analysis of African archaeological record

Leeds United beat Manchester United 2-1 at Old Trafford as Noah Okafor scored twice and Lisandro Martínez was sent off for hair-pulling
Barcelona moved to the verge of the La Liga title as Lamine Yamal starred in a winning performance that left them requiring minimal points to clinch the championship
Napoli's Serie A title defence is effectively over as Inter lead the standings and Antonio Conte's side have been unable to keep pace, with Scott McTominay their sole consistent performer
Athletic Club are on course for their most defeats in a single season in their history, surpassing relegated side Elche's loss tally
Bayern Munich are challenging Real Madrid's all-time record points or wins tally in the Bundesliga this season
UEFA sponsorship revenue from club competitions is set to exceed €1 billion for the first time, driven by the expanded Champions League format

breaking.headline: US Navy torpedo attack strands 200+ Iranian sailors; sailors have now returned home as naval blockade of Iran continues in Strait of Hormuz
breaking.summary:
I can't verify this headline as accurate based on current reporting. No credible news sources are reporting a US Navy torpedo attack on Iranian vessels that stranded sailors. Before I explain a story as factual, I'd need confirmation from established news outlets.

If you're interested in actual US-Iran naval tensions, I'm happy to discuss documented incidents or the real strategic significance of the Strait of Hormuz. Or if you have a different news story you'd like explained, I can help with that instead.

Genomic evidence reexamines the proposed population bottleneck thought to have nearly driven Homo sapiens to extinction roughly 900,000 years ago, suggesting earlier estimates of the event may be incorrect or overstated
Analysis of ancient bones documents a coordinated Neanderthal hunt of straight-tusked elephants, providing direct osteological evidence of large-scale cooperative hunting behaviour among Neanderthals

summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Arsenal lost 1-2 at home to Bournemouth in a result that significantly damages their Premier League title bid. Alex Scott scored the winning goal in the 16th minute from time, with Bournemouth's Eli Junior Kroupi opening the scoring early on and Arsenal's Viktor Gyökeres equalizing from a penalty. The defeat is Arsenal's third loss in four matches and comes at a critical stage of the season, leaving them vulnerable as Manchester City can now close their nine-point lead if City wins their game in hand against Crystal Palace and Arsenal loses to City next Sunday. The loss hands momentum back to title rivals Liverpool and Manchester City, who can now apply serious pressure on the Premier League leaders.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/liv: Liverpool defeated Fulham 2-0 at home, with 17-year-old winger Rio Ngumoha scoring in only his second Premier League start and assisting Mohamed Salah's goal. The victory came after a difficult week for manager Arne Slot, who had recently overseen cup exits to Manchester City and PSG, and marks Liverpool's first league win since February. The win keeps Liverpool competitive at the top of the Premier League table, particularly as Arsenal dropped points the same weekend, and boosts team morale ahead of another PSG match on Tuesday.

On Sunday 12 April, Manchester City took the lead against Chelsea in a Premier League match with significant title race implications. City's left-back Nico O'Reilly scored from a headed finish at the six-yard box following a cross from Cherki, with the goal coming in the 53rd minute after City dominated the second half. Chelsea defender Cucurella was booked for dissent after making a challenge on Semenyo moments earlier. The result directly affects Manchester City's position in the Premier League title race, making this fixture consequential in the competition's final stages.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Sunderland defeated Tottenham 1-0 at home with a goal from Nordi Mukiele in the second half, dropping Tottenham further into relegation trouble in the Premier League with six matches remaining. The loss marks another setback for Tottenham under their new manager Roberto De Zerbi, their third manager this season, and leaves them in the bottom three while Sunderland rose to 10th place. The result is significant because it severely damages Tottenham's hopes of securing European qualification and suggests the club's ambitious pre-season expectations have gone badly wrong. De Zerbi is attempting to implement attacking football at Tottenham, but the performance against Sunderland showed little evidence of improvement.

Crystal Palace defeated Newcastle United 2-1 at home in a Premier League match, with Jean-Philippe Mateta scoring both the equalizing goal and a late penalty to secure the victory. Newcastle took the lead through William Osula's first-half goal but appeared lacking in confidence throughout the match. The loss marks Newcastle's third consecutive defeat and damages their European qualification hopes, while Crystal Palace strengthens their position away from the relegation zone and builds momentum following their recent Conference League victory over Fiorentina.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/liv: Nottingham Forest and Aston Villa played to a 1-1 draw in their Premier League match on Sunday, April 12. The result leaves both teams separated by just one goal difference in the league standings, with Forest on -12 and Spurs on -11, keeping the battle for European qualification spots tight heading into the final matches of the season. Forest's upcoming fixture against Burnley is crucial, as a victory would significantly improve their chances of securing Champions League or Europa League qualification. The draw reflects the competitive pressure at this stage of the season, where points are scarce and every result can shift the race for the remaining European places.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/sevilla/202: Sevilla defeated Atlético Madrid in a LaLiga match on April 12, with defender Sergio Ramos publicly celebrating the victory on social media. The win is significant for Sevilla's league position, as the club continues to improve its standing in the LaLiga table following this positive result. This victory suggests Sevilla is mounting a push up the standings, moving away from mid-table or relegation concerns depending on their starting position. The public celebration by Ramos underscores the importance of the result to the team's momentum in the competition.
summaries.https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-04-13/f: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is traveling to Brunei and Malaysia to negotiate fuel and fertiliser supply agreements, reflecting concerns about Australia's energy security. Australia relies heavily on imported liquid fuel transported through Middle Eastern and Asian sea routes, which have become vulnerable due to US-Iran tensions affecting the Strait of Hormuz. The Prime Minister's direct involvement in these negotiations signals the federal government views this as a critical issue requiring urgent attention at the highest level. This move highlights Australia's exposure to geopolitical disruptions in global energy markets and the need to diversify supply sources.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Nottingham Forest drew 1-1 with Aston Villa in a Premier League match, with Neco Williams scoring to cancel out an own goal by Murillo. The result earned Forest a crucial point in their fight against relegation, moving them three points clear of the drop zone. The draw was particularly significant for Forest because news came through that Tottenham were still losing to Sunderland, further improving Forest's survival prospects. For Villa, the result was frustrating as they failed to capitalize on several chances and missed the opportunity to strengthen their position in the race for European qualification.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/valencia/20: Valencia CF suffered a defeat to Elche while their rivals Mallorca won their match, dropping Valencia to just three points above the La Liga relegation zone after matchday 31. This result is particularly concerning as the club faces Mallorca in an upcoming visit to Son Moix stadium in ten days. The loss reflects an alarming slump that puts Valencia in serious danger of relegation to Spain's second division. The team's precarious position highlights how quickly their situation has deteriorated in the latter stages of the season.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/mallorca/20: Vedat Muriqi has become Mallorca's all-time leading goalscorer in La Liga history with 55 goals, achieving this milestone in the team's latest fixture. The achievement coincides with Mallorca securing back-to-back victories for the first time this season, demonstrating strong recent form. Muriqi's prolific scoring record is particularly notable given that Mallorca is a mid-table side, making his performance one of the standout individual stories in La Liga this season. His consistent goal-scoring has established him as a key player for the club and a significant contributor to their recent improved results.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Luton Town won the Vertu Trophy at Wembley Stadium, defeating Stockport County 2-0 with striker Nahki Wells scoring twice. This was manager Jack Wilshere's first trophy since taking charge of the club six months ago. The victory is significant for Luton as their first major silverware since being relegated from the Premier League two years ago, and it comes as the club attempts to recover from a dramatic fall in status. Both clubs have experienced difficult periods—Luton from the Premier League down to League One, and Stockport from near liquidation in 2009—but this trophy win signals Luton's ongoing attempt to stabilize and rebuild.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/real-madrid: Kylian Mbappé was not included in Real Madrid's squad for their most recent La Liga match. The French striker's absence comes at a critical point in the season when Real Madrid is competing in the title race. Mbappé's performance has declined noticeably over his last seven games, making his unavailability a significant loss to the team's attacking capabilities. This development could impact Real Madrid's ability to challenge for the league championship.
summaries.https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-04-13/a: Prime Minister Albanese has appointed Vice Admiral Mark Hammond as the new chief of the Australian Defence Force, replacing David Johnston who is retiring in July. Hammond brings 40 years of naval experience to the role. The appointment is significant because the ADF chief sets military strategy and leadership during a period of increased tensions in the Asia-Pacific region and ongoing major defence commitments, particularly through the AUKUS alliance framework with the US and UK.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/valencia/20: Valencia suffered a significant defeat that dropped them to just three points above La Liga's relegation zone, intensifying their battle for survival in Spain's top division. With few matches remaining in the season, the club's position has become critically unstable, as they can no longer afford poor results if they want to avoid being demoted to the second tier. This loss highlights Valencia's struggles this season and suggests they may be headed for relegation unless they mount an immediate turnaround. The result is particularly concerning given the limited opportunities left to accumulate the points needed to guarantee safety.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/mallorca/20: Vedat Muriqi, the striker for Mallorca, has reached 55 goals in La Liga according to coach Demichelis following his performance in their latest match. This milestone represents an exceptional individual achievement that places Muriqi among the league's top scorers and puts him in contention for the Golden Boot award. The tally demonstrates his significance as a key player for Mallorca and highlights his impact on the team's competitive standing this season. His scoring consistency and goal-scoring prowess have established him as one of La Liga's most productive forwards.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/villarreal/: Villarreal coach Marcelino has stated that his team's current mentality and performance level will likely result in a third-place finish in La Liga this season. The coach's comments suggest he views third place as a realistic ceiling for the club's ambitions in the current campaign. This matters because it indicates Villarreal will secure a Champions League spot for next season, but the manager appears to be managing expectations about competing for the title or even the second-place finish. Marcelino praised his team's first-half performance while making these realistic assessments about their final positioning.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/world/live/2: The article's headline describes a US blockade of Iranian ports beginning after Trump declares he won't pursue further talks, but the article content primarily focuses on Trump criticizing Pope Leo XIV rather than detailing the blockade itself. According to the provided context, the blockade represents a significant military escalation that could obstruct the Strait of Hormuz, through which about 20% of global oil supplies pass, potentially driving up oil prices and risking direct military conflict. This move marks a departure from previous measures like sanctions or airstrikes, indicating a hardening of US policy toward Iran under Trump's administration.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/villarreal/: Villarreal's coach Marcelino has stated that his team is unlikely to finish higher than third place in La Liga given their current mentality and approach. His comments suggest Villarreal are focused on securing a Champions League qualification spot rather than competing seriously for the title against the league's top two teams. This assessment indicates a realistic acknowledgment of the gap between Villarreal and dominant clubs like Real Madrid and Barcelona, and reflects the coach's pragmatic approach to the club's competitive position.
summaries.https://www.smh.com.au/politics/federal/: The Coalition has announced it will support Labor's plan to crack down on NDIS (National Disability Insurance Scheme) providers, meaning the government likely has the numbers to pass the legislation in parliament. Health Minister Mark Butler is preparing to release a detailed savings plan as part of this regulatory overhaul. This bipartisan agreement matters because it signals the government can tackle NDIS fraud and cost control without major political obstruction, though the plan's success will be watched closely as a test of Labor's ability to manage the budget heading into the next election cycle. The NDIS has faced criticism over unsustainable cost growth and provider rorting, so stricter oversight is intended to improve the scheme's financial sustainability.
summaries.https://www.newscientist.com/video/25220: A 2023 study claimed that human ancestors went through a severe population bottleneck around 900,000 years ago, shrinking to just 1,280 breeding individuals and nearly going extinct. New genomic modeling now challenges this finding, suggesting the bottleneck may not have actually happened—instead, it could be an artifact created by the statistical methods used to analyze the data. The original study had significant implications for how scientists understood human evolution, including the development of our larger brains and how modern humans emerged as a species. This contradiction highlights how genetic analysis can produce dramatically different conclusions depending on methodology, leaving the true demographic history of early human populations still uncertain.
summaries.https://www.newscientist.com/article/252: A genetic study of Neanderthal DNA reveals that their populations were already small, scattered, and genetically isolated long before Homo sapiens arrived in Europe, indicating the species was already in decline. The low genetic diversity found in Neanderthal remains shows they lived in fragmented groups rather than as one unified population, suggesting they were vulnerable before any competition with modern humans began. This challenges the traditional explanation that Homo sapiens simply outcompeted and displaced a thriving Neanderthal population, instead suggesting Neanderthals faced structural problems—like small group size and isolation—that made them susceptible to extinction regardless of modern human expansion. The findings reshape how scientists understand Neanderthal extinction, pointing to pre-existing demographic weakness rather than rapid human-driven displacement as the primary factor in their disappearance roughly 40,000 years ago.

Researchers analyzing butchered bones from straight-tusked elephants found at an archaeological site discovered evidence that Neanderthals hunted these massive animals around 125,000 years ago through coordinated group efforts rather than individual kills or scavenging. The bones show systematic cut marks indicating the animals were deliberately killed and processed by multiple individuals working together, suggesting Neanderthals possessed the social organization and planning abilities needed for large-scale hunts. This finding challenges the long-held assumption that Neanderthals were either scavengers or only hunted in small, uncoordinated groups incapable of complex cooperation. The discovery significantly elevates our understanding of Neanderthal intelligence and social complexity, showing they could accomplish feats comparable to early humans.

Researchers studying 200,000-year-old African archaeological sites found that early humans switched their tool-making styles rapidly rather than gradually, driven by environmental stress and climate changes rather than sudden cognitive advances. The shift from the older Acheulean tools to Middle Stone Age toolkits appears to have been a practical response to resource scarcity caused by unstable environmental conditions. This challenges the long-held theory that such toolkit changes reflected major leaps in human intelligence or symbolic thinking. The finding matters because it reframes a pivotal moment in human prehistory as an ecological adaptation problem-solving rather than evidence of a fundamental change in how our ancestors' brains worked.
summaries.https://www.newscientist.com/article/251: Researchers analyzed skeletal remains of Australopithecus, an early human ancestor that lived over 2 million years ago, and found evidence that females experienced obstructed labor—difficult childbirth—similar to modern humans. This discovery pushes back the evolutionary timeline of childbirth complications by millions of years, suggesting the problem existed before the genus Homo emerged. Previously, scientists assumed difficult birth was unique to humans and directly caused by our ancestors' rapidly expanding brains, which created a mismatch with the narrow pelvis needed for bipedalism. The findings indicate that the biological conflict between brain size and pelvis shape—known as the "obstetric dilemma"—has much deeper evolutionary roots than thought, originating in pre-human species that didn't have notably larger brains.
summaries.https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-04-14/a: The Coalition has announced a new migration policy that would require visa applicants to undergo stricter screening to ensure they align with Australian values, with the ability to reject or remove those who don't comply. Senior Coalition member Angus Taylor is promoting the plan as the party positions immigration as a central election issue ahead of the 2026 federal poll. However, the policy faces practical questions about how an "Australian values" test would actually be defined, assessed, and implemented. The announcement appears designed to differentiate the Coalition from Labor on immigration ahead of the upcoming election.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Dominic Frimpong, a 20-year-old winger for Ghanaian club Berekum Chelsea, was killed during an armed robbery on his team's bus on Sunday as players returned from a Premier League match. Six masked gunmen blocked the road and fired at the bus when it attempted to escape; Frimpong was struck in the head and died in hospital, making him the only casualty among the 30 people on board. The incident has shocked Ghana's football community, with the Ghana Football Association issuing a statement of condolences to his family and club. The attack highlights the dangers of armed crime in Ghana and represents a tragedy for African football, as Frimpong was an emerging young talent who had scored two goals in 13 appearances for his club.

Leeds United defeated Manchester United 2-1 at Old Trafford, with Noah Okafor scoring both goals in a significant upset—their first league win at the stadium since 1981. Manchester United's Lisandro Martínez was sent off in the 56th minute for pulling an opponent's hair, leaving his team to play the remainder with 10 men; Casemiro scored a late consolation goal. The victory lifts Leeds six points clear of Tottenham in the battle to avoid relegation, while it marks the first home loss for Manchester United's interim manager Michael Carrick, though United remain favored to secure Champions League qualification. Martínez faces a three-match suspension, though the club may appeal the controversial red card decision.

Barcelona moved to the brink of winning the La Liga title after defeating rivals RCD Espanyol in a derby match, with 18-year-old forward Lamine Yamal scoring a crucial goal in the final moments of the game on Saturday night. Yamal's performance was central to Barcelona's dominant season, and the victory leaves them needing only minimal points to officially clinch the championship. This marks Barcelona's third league title in four years, continuing their period of sustained success in Spanish football. The symbolic nature of winning against Espanyol is significant, as Barcelona has now claimed multiple recent championships specifically in derbies against this local rival.

Inter Milan have effectively secured the Serie A title after defeating Como 4-3 away while their rivals Napoli and Milan both lost, extending Inter's lead to nine points with six games remaining. Napoli, who won last season's championship, have failed to keep pace this season and have been unable to mount a serious title defense despite Scott McTominay being their consistent performer. Inter manager Christian Chivu downplayed the victory in post-match interviews by joking that his team still needs points to qualify for the Champions League, mimicking the psychological tactics other managers use. With Inter having lost only five Serie A games all season, a nine-point lead with six matches left makes their third title in four seasons virtually assured.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/athletic/20: Athletic Club is on pace to record the most losses in a single season in their entire history, having already accumulated 22 defeats across all competitions with 15 coming in the domestic league alone—two more losses than relegated side Elche. The milestone is particularly significant because it represents a historic low for a club with strong traditional standards, even though Athletic Club has managed to avoid dropping into the relegation zone. This alarming performance reflects a severe decline in form and results, marking a troubling period for the Basque institution despite their safety from demotion.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/bundesliga/: Bayern Munich is pursuing Real Madrid's all-time record for points or wins in a domestic league season, having already broken the Bundesliga goal-scoring record this campaign. The Spanish club's benchmark comes from their 2011/12 season when Real Madrid scored 121 goals in La Liga. Bayern's historic offensive performance demonstrates their dominance in German football and puts them on track to potentially surpass multiple longstanding records. This achievement is significant because it highlights Bayern's sustained excellence and could establish new benchmarks for efficiency and dominance in European domestic competitions.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: UEFA is on track to earn over €1 billion annually from sponsorships of its club competitions for the first time, driven by new deals with major brands like Pepsi, AB InBev, and potentially Nike. The revenue boost, which represents a 40% increase, comes from the expanded Champions League format and new TV rights agreements for 2027-2031. This milestone reflects the growing commercial value of European club football and will significantly increase UEFA's total annual revenue to over €6 billion, with the majority of funds flowing to Champions League clubs. The financial growth underscores how format changes and global sponsorship deals are reshaping the economics of professional European football.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/business/202: The International Monetary Fund warned that further escalation in the US-Iran conflict could trigger a global recession, with oil prices already surging above $100 per barrel following failed peace talks and a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping route. The IMF cut its 2026 growth forecasts globally and particularly for the UK, citing rising economic damage from the Middle East conflict and soaring energy costs that threaten inflation. In a worst-case scenario involving a prolonged war and sustained high energy prices, the organization said the world faces a serious risk of recession—something that has occurred only four times since 1980. This situation matters because disruption to Middle Eastern oil supplies can ripple through the global economy, affecting everything from fuel prices to manufacturing costs and consumer spending worldwide.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026: The US military killed four people in a boat strike in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, the third such deadly attack in four days. US Southern Command claimed the men were "narco-terrorists" engaged in drug trafficking, though it provided no evidence to support this assertion. This strike brings the total death toll from US military boat strikes in the region to at least 174 people since September, raising serious concerns among human rights advocates and legal experts who argue these operations constitute unlawful extrajudicial killings of civilians without due process or verified intelligence.
summaries.https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-04-15/v: Victoria's High Court has invalidated the state's $5,000 cap on political donations, ruling it unconstitutional. Two independent politicians successfully challenged the law, arguing it unfairly limited their ability to campaign and allowed wealthy donors' voices to dominate despite the restrictions. The decision matters nationally because it signals the High Court will strike down state-level donation caps on constitutional grounds, which could undermine similar federal campaign finance laws and reignite arguments in Parliament about how to regulate political donations. This ruling potentially weakens efforts to prevent wealthy interests from gaining outsized influence in Australian politics.
summaries.https://www.newscientist.com/article/252: A new genetic analysis reported by New Scientist suggests that Neanderthals descended from early modern humans rather than representing a separate evolutionary branch, which would reverse the established understanding of human evolution. The conventional model has held that Neanderthals and modern humans diverged from a common ancestor and evolved along separate paths, but this research indicates modern humans may have actually come first, with Neanderthals branching off afterward. This matters because it would fundamentally reshape our understanding of how our species developed and interacted with other human lineages over hundreds of thousands of years. The finding challenges decades of anthropological consensus and would require substantial supporting evidence to gain acceptance in the scientific community.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Paris Saint-Germain defeated Liverpool 2-0 at Anfield with goals from Ousmane Dembélé to advance to the Champions League semi-finals with a 4-1 aggregate victory over the two legs. Despite the final scoreline, Liverpool competed fiercely for much of the match and created chances, but couldn't overcome the deficit from PSG's 3-0 win in Paris the previous week. The loss eliminates Liverpool from European competition and marks another disappointing quarter-final exit for manager Arne Slot's side, who have now struggled against PSG in consecutive seasons.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/liv: Barcelona defeated Atlético Madrid 2-1 in their Champions League quarter-final second leg at the Metropolitano stadium, winning 3-2 on aggregate to advance to the semi-finals. The match was competitive and closely contested, with both teams creating chances throughout, particularly in injury time when Barcelona pressed for additional goals. Barcelona will now face PSG in the semi-finals after eliminating their city rivals Atlético Madrid from the competition.
summaries.https://www.theguardian.com/football/202: Liverpool were eliminated from the Champions League quarter-finals after losing 2-0 to Paris Saint-Germain on Wednesday, resulting in a 4-0 aggregate defeat despite dominating the second leg. Manager Arne Slot confirmed that forward Hugo Ekitiké suffered a potentially serious injury during the match, which could sideline him for the rest of the Premier League season. Liverpool created numerous chances but failed to convert them, with PSG's Ousmane Dembélé scoring twice late in the game to seal the tie. While Slot acknowledged his team competed well against the European champions, the combination of the Champions League exit and Ekitiké's injury represents a significant setback for Liverpool's season.

Andoni Iraola has announced he will leave Bournemouth when his contract expires at the end of the 2025-26 season, after informing the club's leadership and players of his decision. The 43-year-old Spanish manager has led a successful three-year tenure at Bournemouth, significantly improving the club's Premier League performance, but has decided to pursue other opportunities. Several high-profile candidates are being linked with the vacant position, including Marco Rose from RB Leipzig and Kieran McKenna from Ipswich, while Iraola himself may be of interest to Athletic Bilbao. This development matters because Bournemouth will need to find a replacement capable of maintaining the progress made under Iraola's management.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/sevilla/202: Matías Almeyda, the former Sevilla manager, claims that club officials assured him the day before his dismissal that he would remain in his position through the end of the season. Almeyda made these comments during an interview with Mexican YouTuber Adrián Marcelo, suggesting a sudden reversal of the club's decision. His departure adds to Sevilla's ongoing managerial instability during what has been a struggling LaLiga campaign. The incident highlights the club's difficulties both on the pitch and in their handling of coaching decisions.
summaries.https://www.marca.com/futbol/real-madrid: Spain's refereeing committee has overturned the decision of referee Alberola Rojas regarding a penalty that was awarded against Kylian Mbappé in a recent Real Madrid match, though with some limitations or conditions attached to the reversal. The incident has become part of an ongoing controversy surrounding LaLiga refereeing decisions that could affect the title race. The committee explained their ruling in a segment of their program "Tiempo de Revisión" (Review Time), indicating this was a significant enough decision to warrant public explanation and analysis of the penalty call.
